sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Same animation - clicking on different cells of the top-left grid still highlights the same 4 corner cells of the bottom-right grid. So it seems like clicking anywhere on the top-left grid highlights the 4 corners of the bottom-right grid.
Let me try clicking on the top-right grid and bottom-left grid to see if they highlight different cells.
Actually, let me step back and think about this differently. Maybe the gray border on the bottom-right grid is the key. The 4 corners of the bottom-right grid have gray corner pieces as part of the border. Maybe clicking on the top grids is meant to "copy" the pattern from that grid to the bottom-right.
But wait - the animation only highlights 4 corner cells and doesn't actually change anything. Maybe I need to click on the bottom-right grid itself after selecting a source grid?
Or maybe the goal is simpler than I think. Let me reconsider:
- The bottom-right grid has a gray border (making it the target)
- All outer cells are currently blue (I changed some to red)
- The other 3 grids have different outer color patterns
- Clicking on outer cells toggles them between blue and red
- Clicking on other grids highlights corners of bottom-right (but doesn't change anything)
Maybe the goal is to match ALL four grids to have the same outer pattern? Or maybe I need to match the bottom-right to a specific grid based on some rule.
Let me try clicking on the top-right and bottom-left grids to see if they highlight different cells.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model: Clicking anywhere on top-left grid always highlights same 4 corners of bottom-right. Need to test if other grids highlight different cells. Let me try top-right and bottom-left grids.
